head_coach.name: Mike LaFleur
head_coach.scheme_philosophy: Shanahan/McVay outside zone system; play-action heavy with heavy pre-snap motion, jet sweeps, RPOs; emphasizes outside run game and early easy reads for QB; will call plays

offensive_coordinator.name: Nathaniel Hackett
offensive_coordinator.scheme_tree: Shanahan/LaFleur West Coast zone; formerly OC under Matt LaFleur in Green Bay (2019-21), head coach in Denver (2022), OC with Jets (2023-24)

defensive_coordinator.name: Nick Rallis
defensive_coordinator.scheme_tree: Zimmer/Gannon hybrid; quality control under Mike Zimmer in Minnesota (2018-20), LBs coach under Jonathan Gannon in Philadelphia (2021-22), DC in Arizona since 2023; Fangio-adjacent quarters-heavy system

offense.base_personnel: 11 (shifting toward more 12/13 per McVay/Rams recent evolution)
offense.run_scheme: outside zone / wide zone primary (major shift from prior regime which ran just 10% outside zone in 2025; LaFleur historically emphasizes outside zone)
offense.pass_concepts: play action heavy off outside zone, boot concepts, flood routes, vertical shots off play action, RPOs, jet sweep action; system designed for early easy QB reads
offense.te_usage: elite receiving weapon; Trey McBride set NFL TE single-season receptions record (126 catches, 1,239 yds, 11 TDs in 2025); offense built around TE as primary pass catcher; LaFleur's Rams featured 13-personnel heavily in 2025; expect continued TE-centric passing
offense.rb_usage: bell cow runner in zone scheme; moderate receiving role; Shanahan-tree values backs who can catch but primary function is zone running

defense.alignment: odd front, 3 down linemen with stand-up OLBs; unique three-safety looks frequently deployed on early downs
defense.edge_rush_source: hybrid — OLBs (Josh Sweat, Zaven Collins, Baron Browning, BJ Ojulari) function as primary edge rushers from stand-up and hand-in-dirt alignments; five-man front packages common
defense.coverage_base: Quarters (Cover 4) heavy — led NFL in quarters usage at 30%+ in 2023; three-deep safety shell frequently used with Budda Baker roaming as middle safety; high coverage disguise rate; Fangio-adjacent zone match concepts
defense.blitz_tendency: variable — historically low (19% in 2023) but jumped to heavy (31.8% in 2024, 7th in NFL); expect moderate-to-heavy going forward as talent improves on D-line
defense.lb_role: ILBs primarily coverage/run-fit; OLBs are primary pass rushers; defense values hybrid LBs who can cover and blitz
defense.safety_usage: elite hybrid usage — Budda Baker deployed as box safety, middle safety in 3-high shells, and edge-of-box rover; three-safety alignments are a scheme signature; Dadrion Taylor-Demerson emerging as FS

te_receiving_boost.explanation: Arizona's offense is built around the TE as a primary receiving weapon at an unprecedented level. Trey McBride set the NFL single-season TE receptions record with 126 catches for 1,239 yards and 11 TDs in 2025, earning first-team All-Pro. The prior Petzing offense emphasized 12/13 personnel, and LaFleur's Rams led the NFL in three-TE sets in 2025. McBride functions as a de facto WR1 in this scheme, running routes from inline, slot, and flexed alignments. Any TE prospect with elite receiving traits (route running, hands, separation, YAC) gets maximum value here. The Cardinals explicitly targeted adding another pass-catching TE to complement McBride in 12/13 sets.

lb_pass_rush_boost.explanation: Arizona runs a 3-4 base where OLBs are the primary edge rushers. Josh Sweat, Zaven Collins, Baron Browning, and BJ Ojulari all function as stand-up edge rushers from the OLB position. The scheme deploys five-man fronts and asks OLBs to rush from two-point stances. Any LB prospect with elite pass rush traits (burst, bend, first step, power moves) can be deployed as a full-time edge rusher in this defense. The Zimmer/Gannon defensive lineage also values versatile front-seven players who can rush and drop into coverage.

rb_dual_threat_boost.explanation: The Shanahan/McVay offensive tree values backs who can operate in the passing game as check-down targets and on screen/wheel routes off play action. However, the primary RB function in this outside zone system is as a runner — the back's vision, lateral agility, and ability to press the hole in zone are paramount. James Conner and Trey Benson were both graded well in pass protection (PFF 76.9 and 77.0 respectively in 2024). Receiving ability provides a moderate boost but zone-running traits are the primary filter.

s_hybrid_boost.explanation: Arizona's defense is THE model for hybrid safety usage in the NFL. The scheme deploys three-safety alignments on early downs more than any other team. Budda Baker — an eight-time Pro Bowler ranked as the NFL's top box safety by PFF — plays box, middle safety in 3-high shells, slot coverage, and even blitzes off the edge. He recorded 120+ tackles in 2025 while also covering deep thirds. The defense also morphs CB Garrett Williams into a safety/overhang role. Any safety prospect with box versatility, tackling ability, coverage range, and blitz capability gets premium value in this scheme. Rallis' three-safety structure means the Cardinals literally roster and play more safeties than most teams.

scheme_specific_draft_notes: Arizona is undergoing a massive offensive scheme transition from a run-first power/inside zone system (just 10% outside zone in 2025, lowest in NFL) to LaFleur's Shanahan/McVay outside zone system. This creates acute need for laterally agile offensive linemen who can execute reach blocks — PFF noted none of ARI's 700+ snap OL graded above 60.0 on outside zone in 2025. The OL need may override other picks early. On offense, the TE position carries WR1-level value: McBride's 126-catch, first-team All-Pro season makes ARI the single highest-value landing spot for receiving TEs in the NFL. LaFleur's Rams evolution toward 12/13 personnel means a second receiving TE has real draft value here. Defensively, the 3-4 base with OLBs as primary edge rushers means any LB with pass-rush traits (think Arvell Reese, Sonny Styles types) should be valued at EDGE prices for ARI. The three-safety defense is a defining scheme feature — Rallis literally led the NFL in quarters usage and three-high shells. Safety prospects with box/hybrid versatility (Caleb Downs-type) get a significant value boost, though ARI already has Budda Baker under contract. The blitz rate spiked to 31.8% in 2024, indicating willingness to pressure, which further elevates pass-rushing LB/S hybrid prospects.
